R ~ ( a ) , mifm lorn. 1 9 8 5 m ~ f % . r p d t 3 t h ~ ~ ~ 3 T i ~ ~ i ; m * ~ f % . r p : - MINIWRY O F P E R S O W E 4 PUBLIC GRIEVANCES AND PENSIONS (Department of Penonnel and Training) N E n c A n O N New Delhi, the 9th December. 2009 G.S.R. 874(E).-In exercise ofthe powers conferred by sub-section ( I ) read with clause (c) of sub-section (2) of Section35 and Section 36AoftheAdministrativeTribunals Act, 1985 ( I 3 of 1965), the Central Govemment hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Central Administrative Tribunals (Salaries and Allowances and Conditions of Service of Chairman, Vice-Chairmen and Members) Rules, 1985, namely :- 1. ( I ) These rules may be called the Central Administrative Tribunal (Salaries and Allowances and Conditions ofService of Chairman; Vice-Chairmen and Members) (Second Amendment) Rules, 2009. (2) These rules shall be applicable to the Chairman, ViceChairmen and Members of the Central Administrative Tribunal appointedbefore the 19th February, 2007. (3) They shall be deemed to have come into force witheffect from the 10th August, 1985. Z In rule 8 of the said rules, afier sub-rule (2), the following sub-rule shall be inserted, namely :- A : EXWAORDINARY-. [PARTl l ~ ~ - S ~ c . 3(1)] p~ -- - ~ -... .. - .~ "(3) Gratuity :-The Members who have completed five years of service in the Central Administrative Tribunal shall be entitled for a retirement gratuity equal to one-fourth of the emoluments for each. completed period of six months of service subject to a maximum of sixteen and half'time of the emoluments : Provided that the total amount of gratuity payable to a Member under this clause and the amount of gratuity drawn by him in respect of services rendered in an organisation or department kefore joining Central Administrative Tribunal shall not exceed the maximum amount of gratuity specified by the Central Govemment'for officers of All India Service at the time of his retirement from Government serviee." [F. N0.A-l 1013/37RM)5-ATI C. B. PALIWAL, Jt. Secy. Expbnatov Memorandum The Central Government has decided to gran! retirement gratuity to the Members of the Centra! Administrative Tribunal who were appointed before 19th February, 2007 for the service rendered by them in the Central Administrative Tribunal and accordingly, the Central Administrative Tribunal (Salaries and Allowance and Conditions of Service ofchairman, ViceChaimen and Members) Rules, 1985 are being amended with retrospective effeet. 2. it is certified that nochairman, ViceChaiman and Member ofthe Central Administrative Tribunal is likely to be affected adversely by the proposed amendment being given retrospective effect.
